title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about choosing a present.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Have you bought anything for Anna's baby yet? || Man: I saw a lovely toy elephant in the market, but I'm not sure a baby needs another toy. || Woman: Maybe not. What about clothes? || Man: I don't know the size. || Woman: [8]Then why don't we get a voucher from the children's shop? Her parents can choose what they actually need[/8]. || Man: That's sensible. It isn't very exciting, but it will be useful. || Woman: That makes sense. || Man: Exactly, and I feel much happier about it now.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Have you bought anything for Anna's baby yet?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I saw a lovely toy elephant in the market, but I'm not sure a baby needs another toy.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Maybe not. What about clothes?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I don't know the size.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Then why don't we get a voucher from the children's shop? Her parents can choose what they actually need.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's sensible. It isn't very exciting, but it will be useful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That makes sense.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly, and I feel much happier about it now.

questions.question: What does the woman suggest buying?
questions.explanation: She suggests getting a voucher so the baby's parents can choose what they need.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking to a friend about a photography course.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you enjoying the photography course? || Woman: Yes, though it's harder than I expected. The teacher explains things clearly, and the classes aren't too long. || Man: So what's difficult? || Woman: [9]I'm used to taking quick photos on my phone, but now we have to think about light, distance and background before pressing the button[/9]. || Man: That sounds useful. || Woman: It is. My pictures already look better. || Man: I'm sure that will help. || Woman: I hope so. It's better to sort it out early.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you enjoying the photography course?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, though it's harder than I expected. The teacher explains things clearly, and the classes aren't too long.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So what's difficult?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'm used to taking quick photos on my phone, but now we have to think about light, distance and background before pressing the button.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds useful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is. My pictures already look better.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I'm sure that will help.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I hope so. It's better to sort it out early.

questions.question: What does the woman find difficult about the course?

options.C: staying interested for the whole lesson

questions.explanation: She says she now has to think about several things before taking each photo.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a concert.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Did you enjoy the concert last night? || Man: The band played well, but I was too far from the stage to see much. || Woman: I was near the front, and the view was great. || Man: Lucky you. The tickets weren't cheap either. || Woman: [10]True, but the sound was excellent everywhere in the hall, even right at the back[/10]. || Man: I agree. You could hear every word clearly. || Woman: That sounds fair. || Man: Yes, and I think everyone will understand the situation.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy the concert last night?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The band played well, but I was too far from the stage to see much.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I was near the front, and the view was great.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Lucky you. The tickets weren't cheap either.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: True, but the sound was excellent everywhere in the hall, even right at the back.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. You could hear every word clearly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That sounds fair.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, and I think everyone will understand the situation.

questions.question: What do they both say about the concert?

options.B: The view of the stage was poor.
options.C: The sound quality was very good.

questions.explanation: They agree that the sound was excellent and clear.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling a friend about a new job.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: How's the new job going? || Man: Pretty well. The office is smaller than my old one, but everyone is friendly. || Woman: Are the hours okay? || Man: Mostly. I start earlier, which was hard at first. [11]The best thing is that I can walk there in ten minutes instead of spending an hour on the bus[/11]. || Woman: That must make mornings easier. || Man: It really does. || Woman: I hadn't thought of that. || Man: Neither had I at first, but now it seems obvious.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: How's the new job going?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Pretty well. The office is smaller than my old one, but everyone is friendly.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Are the hours okay?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Mostly. I start earlier, which was hard at first. The best thing is that I can walk there in ten minutes instead of spending an hour on the bus.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That must make mornings easier.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It really does.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I hadn't thought of that.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Neither had I at first, but now it seems obvious.

questions.question: What does the man like best about his new job?
questions.explanation: He says the best thing is being able to walk there quickly instead of taking a long bus journey.

extracts.context: You will hear two people talking about a school trip.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you helping with the school trip to the castle? || Woman: Yes, but I'm a bit nervous. I've taken children to museums before, but never somewhere with so many outdoor areas. || Man: The weather should be fine. || Woman: That's good. [12]My main worry is keeping everyone together, because the castle grounds are huge and the children get excited easily[/12]. || Man: You'll have plenty of other adults there. || Woman: I hope so. || Man: That must be useful. || Woman: It is, especially when there are several things to think about.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you helping with the school trip to the castle?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, but I'm a bit nervous. I've taken children to museums before, but never somewhere with so many outdoor areas.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: The weather should be fine.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: That's good. My main worry is keeping everyone together, because the castle grounds are huge and the children get excited easily.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You'll have plenty of other adults there.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I hope so.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That must be useful.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is, especially when there are several things to think about.

questions.question: What is the woman worried about?

options.A: whether the weather will be suitable

questions.explanation: She says her main worry is keeping the children together in the large castle grounds.

extracts.context: You will hear a man talking about a cooking programme.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker: I watched a cooking programme yesterday where two chefs had to make dinner from leftovers. Some of the dishes looked strange at first, and I wouldn't try all of them. But [13]what impressed me most was how simple the ideas were. They used ordinary food that most people already have at home[/13]. The programme didn't make me want to become a chef, but it did make me think before throwing food away.

dialogue.speaker: speaker
dialogue.text: I watched a cooking programme yesterday where two chefs had to make dinner from leftovers. Some of the dishes looked strange at first, and I wouldn't try all of them. But what impressed me most was how simple the ideas were. They used ordinary food that most people already have at home. The programme didn't make me want to become a chef, but it did make me think before throwing food away.

questions.question: What did the man like about the programme?

options.A: The recipes were easy to use.

questions.explanation: He says the ideas were simple and used ordinary food people already have at home.
